name: ScrapeOps
pricing_notes: Free: 1K pages/mo; Starter $49/mo; Scale $149/mo; per-page pricing

performance.notes: Varies by target site and JS rendering; JavaScript rendering adds 3-8s

agent_readiness.notes: Web scraping API with proxy rotation, JS rendering, and anti-bot bypass. Fake user agents, geo-targeting, and residential proxies. Good Scrapy integration. Scraping monitoring/analytics dashboard. Cheaper than Zyte/BrightData.

gotchas: Anti-bot bypass not 100% reliable for Cloudflare/Imperva protected sites
JS rendering adds significant latency and cost
No structured extraction (returns raw HTML, not parsed data)
Rate limits and retries must be handled by the caller
best_when: Agents that need to collect data from multiple web sources without APIs
Cost-effective web scraping alternative to BrightData/Zyte
Building data collection pipelines for AI training or RAG
avoid_when: Sites with sophisticated bot protection (use Zyte or SmartProxy)
Need structured extracted data (use Diffbot or Firecrawl)
summary: ScrapeOps provides affordable web scraping infrastructure with proxy rotation, JS rendering, and basic anti-bot bypass. More cost-effective than BrightData or Zyte. Scrapy integration is a notable strength. Returns raw HTML — teams needing structured data extraction should pair with Diffbot or Firecrawl. Best for budget-conscious data collection pipelines.
